title: item.setStorageLocation: recompute expiration for the chosen location
description: Option A (user decision): location is silent at import; users correct it from item detail. Mutation {id, storageLocation} recomputes expiresAt from receipt.purchasedAt + the ItemType's per-location seconds, source FOODKEEPER (an explicit location change supersedes a USER date — the user is asking for the estimate for that location). No FoodKeeper data for that location -> location updates, date/source unchanged. Future recomputed date un-expires EXPIRED items.
close_reason: setStorageLocation verified locally: FRIDGE/USER Jul 8 -> FREEZER/FOODKEEPER Sep 26 (purchase+90d); no-data locations leave date untouched; future recompute un-expires.

title: internal.expireItems for the scheduler
description: internalProcedure: flip caller-independent ACTIVE items with expiresAt < now to EXPIRED (resolvedAt stays null — expiry is not a user outcome). Returns count. Token-gated like internal.ping; broccoli-scheduler calls it on cron (scheduler work tracked in its own repo beads).
close_reason: internal.expireItems flips ACTIVE past-due to EXPIRED (resolvedAt stays null), token-gated (401 without), idempotent, returns count. Verified.

title: item.adjustExpiration: user-set dates
description: Mutation {id, expiresAt} scoped to caller; sets source USER. PRD wants adjustments remembered for next time — v1: store only on the item; per-user learned overrides filed as follow-up when there's data.
close_reason: item.adjustExpiration sets USER source; future date un-expires EXPIRED (never eaten/tossed). Verified.

title: Compute expiration at save; serve it from item.list
description: receipt.confirm: link each item to ItemType by snapped category, choose default storage (fridge if fridgeSeconds present, else pantry, else freezer), compute expiresAt from receipt.purchasedAt (fallback now) using the location lifespan (use max of min/max range midpoint — decide and document), set source FOODKEEPER. item.list drops the runtime lifespan join and returns expiresAt/source/storageLocation directly; keep response shape flat-aliased.
close_reason: receipt.confirm links items to ItemType and stamps expiresAt (fridge>pantry>freezer default, from purchase date, FOODKEEPER source); item.list now includes EXPIRED items; legacy lifespan join kept for pre-Phase-3 rows.

title: Schema+seed: per-location lifespans, Item expiration fields, EXPIRED status
description: item_type: pantrySeconds/fridgeSeconds/freezerSeconds (nullable) + per-location tips, derived in the seed from the raw FoodKeeper JSON (Metric: Days/Weeks/Months/Years; blank = not applicable; 'When Ripe' etc -> null). Item: itemTypeId FK (nullable), storageLocation enum PANTRY/FRIDGE/FREEZER (nullable), expiresAt DateTime?, expirationSource enum FOODKEEPER/LLM/USER (nullable). ItemStatus += EXPIRED. Migration + idempotent re-seed.
close_reason: Per-location lifespans + tips derived from raw FoodKeeper in seed (364 fridge/302 pantry/342 freezer of 661); Item gains itemTypeId FK, storageLocation, expiresAt, expirationSource; ItemStatus += EXPIRED; seed switched to name-keyed upserts for stable ids; migration applied locally.

title: [epic] Phase 3 — Expiration advice (broccoli-api)
description: Real expiration advice replacing Phase 2's client-side estimate (PRD §7). Every saved item gets expiresAt + a visible source; storage location matters; users can adjust; the scheduler expires items over time. Groundwork in place: FoodKeeper seeded with full raw records (Pantry/Refrigerate/Freeze Min/Max + Metric + tips), category names already snap to ItemType. Done when: every saved item carries an expiration estimate with source, adjustable, and ACTIVE items flip to EXPIRED on schedule.

title: tRPC item router: list / resolve / unresolve
description: item.list: caller's ACTIVE items on SAVED receipts, newest first, with receipt storeName+purchasedAt for context (inventory + check-in deck both read this). item.resolve({id, outcome: EATEN|TOSSED}): sets status + resolvedAt, scoped to caller. item.unresolve({id}): back to ACTIVE (undo). Annotate resolver outputs with flat aliases (TS2589 lesson from Phase 1).
close_reason: item.list / item.resolve / item.unresolve implemented with flat InventoryItem output alias; typecheck clean.

title: Schema: ItemStatus (ACTIVE/EATEN/TOSSED) + resolvedAt on Item
description: Add ItemStatus enum and Item.status (default ACTIVE) + Item.resolvedAt DateTime? (when the user swiped it). Migration via prisma migrate dev against local broccoli_api. Index on (userId, status) for inventory queries. EXPIRED arrives in Phase 3 — don't add it yet.
close_reason: ItemStatus enum + status/resolvedAt on Item, composite (userId,status) index replacing the single-column one; migration applied to local broccoli_api.

title: [epic] Phase 2 — Inventory & daily check-in (broccoli-api)
description: API side of Phase 2 (PRD §7): give items a lifecycle so the app can show a live inventory and record eaten-vs-tossed outcomes — the waste-data backbone (PRD §1 north star). Design: ItemStatus enum ACTIVE/EATEN/TOSSED (scheduler adds expiry transitions in Phase 3); inventory = items on SAVED receipts with status ACTIVE; single-item resolve mutation with undo (swipe mistakes must be reversible). Done when: the app can list a user's inventory and flip items to eaten/tossed (and back) via tRPC, with outcomes persisted for the waste metric.
close_reason: Phase 2 api surface complete and verified: inventory listing + check-in resolve/undo.

title: Set missing Railway env vars on broccoli-api (UPLOADTHING_TOKEN, BROCCOLI_MODEL_URL, BROCCOLI_MODEL_API_KEY)
description: railway variables --service broccoli-api shows none of UPLOADTHING_TOKEN, BROCCOLI_MODEL_URL, BROCCOLI_MODEL_API_KEY are set, so deployed uploads and receipt parsing cannot work. Found during 2r8.6 verification. Set them in the Railway dashboard (model URL should use the private domain; API key must match broccoli-model's API_KEY).
close_reason: User set UPLOADTHING_TOKEN, BROCCOLI_MODEL_URL, BROCCOLI_MODEL_API_KEY on the Railway broccoli-api service (verified via railway variables 2026-07-02).

title: tRPC: receipt.create / get / confirm + async parse
description: receipt.create: persist Receipt(PROCESSING), fire-and-forget parse (call broccoli-model with image URL + itemTypes), return receiptId. receipt.get: poll status + items. receipt.confirm: save user-corrected items, set SAVED. Includes price '$12.98'->float parsing and category->ItemType mapping.

title: Make broccoli-model stateless (accept itemTypes in /ocr)
description: Refactor broccoli-model /ocr to accept the ItemType catalog in the request payload instead of reading Postgres, and stop writing the OCR-text Receipt row. Honors 'broccoli-api is the single writer'. api passes the catalog it owns. Keep x-api-key auth.
close_reason: Model-side refactor complete & merged in broccoli-model#11: /ocr is stateless (accepts itemTypes:[{name,category}] in payload, no Receipt write, no core-DB read, x-api-key kept). API-side catalog-passing when calling /ocr is tracked under 2r8.5.

comments.author: Afrothundr
comments.text: Model-side refactor done: broccoli-model /ocr now stateless — accepts itemTypes:[{name,category}] in payload, no Receipt write, no core-DB read, x-api-key kept. PR: Afrothundr/broccoli-model#11. Remaining for this task = none on model side; API passes the catalog when the /ocr call is wired in 2r8.5.

title: Wire UploadThing for receipt images
description: Server-side UploadThing so the app can upload a receipt photo and the api gets a fetchable URL + key. Store url/key on Receipt; key enables later deletion. broccoli-model fetches the URL.
close_reason: Wired UploadThing (v7 fetch adapter). New src/uploadthing.ts defines the receiptImage FileRouter (image, 8MB, 1 file) with a better-auth-gated middleware (rejects unauthenticated) and onUploadComplete returning {uploadedBy,url,key}. Mounted on Hono at ALL /api/uploadthing; OurFileRouter type exported via package './uploadthing' for the mobile client. UPLOADTHING_TOKEN documented in .env.example + README. Receipt persistence with url/key is deferred to receipt.create (2r8.5). Verified: typecheck clean; server boots; GET /api/uploadthing returns the receiptImage route config.

title: Seed ItemType from USDA FoodKeeper (CC0)
description: ETL the FoodKeeper JSON (data.gov, CC0) into ItemType: map name/category, collapse location-aware ranges to a single representative lifespan for now, set source='FoodKeeper', stash the raw FoodKeeper record in raw Json. Idempotent upsert seed script. ~400 items. Note: clinical names make the parser pick-list noisier — track as a tuning follow-up.
close_reason: Seeded 661 ItemTypes from USDA FoodKeeper (CC0). Vendored faithful source JSON (prisma/seeds/foodkeeper.json); seed.ts derives name (subtitle-disambiguated, all unique), category (14 top-level), representative lifespan (collapsed from FoodKeeper timeframes; Indefinitely->10y; 10 durationless items left 0 for Phase 3 LLM fallback), storageAdvice, source, raw. Idempotent (deleteMany+createMany). Verified against ephemeral Postgres.

title: Prisma models + migration: Receipt, Item, ItemType
description: Add Receipt (userId, imageUrl, imageKey, status enum PROCESSING/READY/SAVED/ERROR, storeName, purchasedAt, total, rawExtraction Json), Item (receiptId, userId, name, price, quantity, unit, category), and ItemType (name unique, category, suggestedLifeSpanSeconds, storageAdvice, source, raw Json). Location-agnostic for now; raw holds the FoodKeeper record for Phase 3 expansion. Generate migration.
close_reason: Added Receipt, Item, ItemType models + ReceiptStatus enum; migration 20260630172917_add_receipt_item_itemtype verified against ephemeral Postgres (tables, FKs, indexes present); typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1 — Capture → extract → review (broccoli-api)
description: API side of the core loop: receive a receipt image, run it through broccoli-model, persist extracted items, and let the app save a corrected list. Decisions locked: images on UploadThing; parsing async + poll; broccoli-model made stateless (api passes ItemType catalog); ItemType seeded from USDA FoodKeeper (CC0), location-agnostic schema for now (single lifespan + storageAdvice + source + raw JSON), expand to pantry/fridge/freezer at Phase 3. Done when: a real receipt becomes saved, corrected Items in Postgres via broccoli-api.
close_reason: All 6 children done; Phase 1 core loop verified API-side end to end (see 2r8.6).

title: Deploy broccoli-api to Railway (start + migrate-on-release)
description: Railway service: start command 'npm start', release runs 'npm run db:deploy'. Confirm public URL serves /health.
notes: Service broccoli-api created (repo-linked, domain https://broccoli-api-production.up.railway.app, all env vars set, railway.json migrate-on-release). BLOCKED: no GitHub webhook installed — auto-deploy needs the Railway GitHub App authorized in the dashboard (browser). Alt: one-time 'railway up' to verify build now.
close_reason: Deployed & verified on Railway. Root cause of first failure: Railway used Node 18, incompatible with tsx --env-file-if-exists; pinned engines.node>=22. Live at https://broccoli-api-production.up.railway.app — /health, /trpc/health, /api/auth/* all 200; internal.ping correctly UNAUTHORIZED without token. GitHub auto-deploy confirmed.

title: Run initial Prisma migration against Railway Postgres
description: npm run db:migrate locally to create migration, then db:deploy on Railway. broccoli-api owns all migrations.
close_reason: Done: 0_init migration (better-auth tables) generated via migrate diff + applied via migrate deploy to broccoli_api. Verified 5 tables present.

title: Provision Railway Postgres + Redis; set env vars
description: Create Railway project services for Postgres and Redis. Set DATABASE_URL, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL, INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN. Needs interactive 'railway login'.
close_reason: Done: fresh broccoli_api DB created in existing core-db instance (template0, to dodge collation mismatch); Redis already present; DATABASE_URL (private), BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL, INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN set on the broccoli-api service.

title: Scaffold service: Hono + tRPC + Prisma + better-auth
description: Node/TS scaffold with /health, /api/auth/*, tRPC router (health/me/internal.ping), token-gated internalProcedure for scheduler calls.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 0 — Foundations (broccoli-api)
description: Stand up broccoli-api: scaffold, Railway deploy with fresh Postgres, better-auth, and a health/auth surface the mobile app can reach. Done when the Expo app can authenticate and hit /health.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete: broccoli-api scaffolded, deployed to Railway with Postgres, better-auth for mobile, /health + auth verified reachable from a device.

title: Verify Phase 3 api end to end
description: Local: re-seed; save a receipt; items get expiresAt+FOODKEEPER source per storage default; unmatched item gets LLM estimate flagged; adjustExpiration flips to USER; internal.expireItems expires a past-due item; item.list carries it all.
close_reason: Local e2e: confirm stamped bananas/eggs/tuna with correct locations+dates; uncategorized untouched; adjust->USER; sweep expired the past-due banana; list carried EXPIRED; future-date adjust reactivated it.

title: LLM fallback for unmatched items
description: Items with no ItemType match (category null/Unknown) get a Gemini structured-output estimate (days + suggested storage), clearly stored as source LLM. Run async after confirm (don't block save). Reuse the AI_KEY pattern; consider calling via broccoli-model vs direct — pick simplest that keeps api deployable and document why.

title: Verify Phase 2 api surface end to end
description: Against local api+db: saved receipt's items appear in item.list; resolve EATEN and TOSSED flip status+resolvedAt and drop from list; unresolve restores; cross-user access 404s; unauthenticated 401s.
close_reason: Verified against local api+db: 17 ACTIVE items listed with receipt context; resolve EATEN/TOSSED set status+resolvedAt and dropped from list; unresolve restored ACTIVE and cleared resolvedAt; unauth 401; unknown id NOT_FOUND.

title: Verify core loop API-side end to end
description: With a real receipt image URL, exercise create->parse->get(READY)->confirm and confirm Items persist correctly in Postgres. API side of Phase 1 'done when'.
close_reason: Verified end to end locally on main: signed-up better-auth user; receipt.create with a real Whole Foods receipt (public UploadThing URL) -> PROCESSING; background parse via local broccoli-model (Vision OCR + Gemini) -> READY with 17 items, store name, prices, ItemType-snapped categories, rawExtraction stored, total summed from line prices; receipt.confirm with corrections (renamed item, removed item, hand-added item, set purchasedAt/total) -> SAVED; all rows verified directly in Postgres; unauthenticated access correctly 401s. Required fixing retired gemini-2.0-flash in broccoli-model (PR #13). Follow-ups filed: Railway env vars missing on broccoli-api; tRPC stack-trace leak; ocrUrl error swallowing (broccoli-model).

title: Verify /health + auth reachable from a device
description: From broccoli-mobile dev build, hit /health and complete a sign-in against the deployed broccoli-api. This is the api side of Phase 0 'done when'.
close_reason: Verified from Android dev build: /health reachable and sign-in completes against deployed broccoli-api (Railway).

title: Configure better-auth for mobile sessions
description: Verify email/password sign-up + session works; tune session expiry for long-lived mobile sessions; set trustedOrigins for the app.
close_reason: Registered server-side @better-auth/expo expo() plugin (pairs with mobile expoClient), tuned sessions to 30d expiry/1d updateAge, kept broccolimobile:// trustedOrigins. Verified end-to-end against ephemeral Postgres: sign-up/email creates user, session cookie authorizes tRPC me, expiresAt confirms 30d window, no-cookie returns UNAUTHORIZED.

title: Don't leak stack traces in tRPC error responses
description: Unauthorized/tRPC errors currently return the full server stack trace in the JSON body (seen during 2r8.6 verification). Strip 'stack' from error shape in production via errorFormatter/onError.
